commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The blocker has a narrow mechanical repair that can preserve the intended behavior, existing tests, and supplied live proof without requiring a product decision.; Needs attention: The retry creates a concrete validation-to-use gap at the untrusted MCP server boundary.; Review finding: [P1] Validate the same tools/list response that you catalog â€” src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts:358-360 (sha=6630ba75161d82708d22dd982283c478b240ec05)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a narrowly gated lenient MCP tool-list fallback, backfills missing root object types, filters explicit non-object schemas, preserves SDK metadata caching, and expands runtime regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +138, Tests +325. Total +463 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main calls the pinned SDK's strict `listTools()` parser, whose ToolSchema requires root `inputSchema.type` to be `object`; the supplied real stdio output independently demonstrates that this drops the whole server catalog.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocker has a narrow mechanical repair that can preserve the intended behavior, existing tests, and supplied live proof without requiring a product decision.

target.title: fix: accept valid MCP tool schemas without strict inputSchema.type check
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes MCP server loading failure when tools have `inputSchema` without explicit `type: "object"`.

Closes #63602

## Changes
- `listAllTools` tries the strict SDK `client.listTools()` first, so the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` side effects (output-schema validators, task-support guards consumed by `callTool()`) are preserved for spec-compliant servers.
- The lenient re-list (`LenientListToolsResultSchema` via `client.request()`) is taken **only** when every Zod issue in the strict failure is the #63602 shape â€” path `["tools", <n>, "inputSchema", "type"]`. Any other strict failure (invalid `outputSchema`, task metadata, malformed envelope) keeps rejecting the server, so the fallback cannot launder unrelated invalid fields into the catalog (`isRootInputSchemaTypeValidationError`).
- After a lenient page is fetched, the fallback calls the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` with the listed tools, restoring exact parity with the strict path: output-schema validators and task-required guards are enforced by `callTool()` for fallback-listed tools too. The call is an optional invocation through a narrow local type, degrading to uncached metadata if a future SDK removes the method.
- `normalizeInputSchema()` backfills `type: "object"` only when the field is missing, preserving any explicit type the server provides.
- `isValidRootInputSchema()` + a catalog gate (`collectValidCatalogTools`) skip tools whose root `inputSchema.type` is explicitly non-object (e.g. `"string"`, `"array"`) and emit a `logWarn`. Server `toolCount` reports accepted tools only.

## Root Cause
The MCP SDK v1.29.0 `ToolSchema` validates `inputSchema.type` as `z.literal("object")`. Some MCP servers (especially older SDKs or custom implementations) omit `type` from `inputSchema`, returning `{ properties: { ... } }`, which trips a Zod validation error: `invalid_value expected "object" in inputSchema.type` and crashes the whole catalog.

## v5 notes (current revision)
Addresses both P2 risks from the latest ClawSweeper review:
- **Fallback gate narrowed**: previously any Zod validation error triggered the lenient re-list; now only failures where *every* issue is the root `inputSchema.type` check do. Regression tests prove a malformed `outputSchema` (alone or mixed with a repairable typeless tool) keeps the server rejected.
- **`cacheToolMetadata()` parity on the fallback path**: lenient pages now feed the SDK metadata cache, so `callTool()` enforces output-schema validation ("has an output schema but did not return structured content", structuredContent mismatch) and task-required guards identically on both paths. Behavioral test included; all three new behavioral tests fail when either fix is reverted (mutation-checked).

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Bundle MCP tool cataloging accepts tools whose root `inputSchema` omits `type` via a narrowly-gated lenient fallback, backfills `type: "object"`, skips tools with explicit non-object root schemas, keeps servers with unrelated strict failures rejected, and preserves the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` side effects on both the strict and fallback paths.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from the rebased PR head, Node 25.2.1, pnpm 11.2.2, driving the real bundle MCP runtime (`getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime` â†’ `getCatalog()` â†’ `callTool()`) against three real out-of-process stdio MCP servers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on both files, plus a live three-server e2e script via `pnpm exec tsx`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  40 passed (40)
pnpm tsgo:core / tsgo:core:test: clean
oxlint / oxfmt --check: clean

e2e (three live stdio servers):
tools: ["legacySdkServer/search_docs"]            // typeless inputSchema repaired via fallback
servers: { "legacySdkServer": { "toolCount": 1 } } // bad_array_tool skipped with logWarn
diagnostics: badOutputServer + mixedServer rejected (strict failure not at inputSchema.type)
callTool(search_docs) â†’ McpError -32600: Tool search_docs has an output schema but did not
  return structured content                        // metadata cache populated on fallback path
```

Observed result after fix: A `tools/list` payload whose `inputSchema` omits `type` catalogs cleanly with `type: "object"` backfilled; a sibling with explicit `type: "array"` is skipped with a logWarn; a server with a malformed `outputSchema` â€” alone or mixed with a repairable tool â€” stays rejected instead of being laundered through the fallback; `callTool()` on a fallback-listed tool enforces its `outputSchema` exactly as the strict path would.

What was not tested: A live third-party MCP server connected to an actual model-provider tool call was not tested in this environment.
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
